docs.title: Reinforcement Learning for Automated Discrete Choice Model Specification
docs.text:
Delphos is a multitask reinforcement learning agent designed to assist the discrete choice model specification process. The framework formulates model specification as a sequential decision-making problem in which Delphos progressively proposes utility specifications and receives feedback from an estimation environment through metrics such as Log-Likelihood, AIC, BIC, and convergence diagnostics.

Delphos is designed to support both methodological research in reinforcement learning for choice modelling and the practical application of trained agents for assisted model specification.

docs.title: Delphos Core
docs.text:
Main research and development repository containing the complete reinforcement learning framework required to train, evaluate, and extend Delphos.

Includes:

  • Markov Decision Process formulation
  • State and action spaces
  • Reward functions
  • Task manager
  • Reinforcement learning agents
  • Environment and Apollo integration
  • Training pipelines
  • Inference modules
  • Benchmarking utilities
  • Experiment configurations

docs.title: Delphos User
docs.text:
Lightweight repository for applying trained Delphos agents in inference mode to unseen or similar discrete choice modelling contexts without requiring further training.

Includes:

  • Loading pre-trained weights
  • Loading dataset schemas
  • Utility specification generation
  • Pareto-front generation
  • Inference workflows
  • Reproducible tutorials
  • Deployment examples

docs.title: Transport Choice Datasets
docs.text:
Repository of standardized transport choice datasets used for training, inference, benchmarking, and reproducibility.

Includes:

  • Raw and processed datasets
  • Metadata and schemas
  • Variable dictionaries
  • Benchmark tasks
  • Dataset preprocessing pipelines
  • Reproducibility resources

docs.title: Reinforcement Learning for Model Specification
docs.text: Delphos formulates utility specification as a sequential decision-making problem in which an agent progressively builds and evaluates utility specifications. Moreover, Delphos can propose utility specifications based on modelling strategies learned from previous datasets and experiments.

docs.title: Multi-Task Learning
docs.text: Delphos supports training across multiple transport choice datasets to learn transferable modelling strategies rather than dataset-specific heuristics.

docs.title: Reward Functions
docs.text:
Reward functions may incorporate:

  • goodness-of-fit,
  • model complexity,
  • convergence quality,
  • parameter significance,
  • and behavioural plausibility constraints.

docs.title: Apollo Integration
docs.text: The framework integrates with the Apollo R package for estimation and evaluation of generated specifications.

docs.title: Reproducibility and Benchmarking
docs.text: The ecosystem includes standardized datasets, benchmark tasks, and reproducible workflows for evaluating reinforcement learning approaches for discrete choice model specification.

docs.title: Repository Structure
docs.text:
Multitask-Delphos/
│
├── docs/
├── tutorials/
├── examples/

│   ├── delphos-user/
│   └── transport-choice-datasets/
│

├── README.md
└── CONTRIBUTING.md

docs.title: Citation
docs.text: If you use Delphos, please cite the corresponding repositories and associated publications.

docs.title: Transport Choice Datasets
docs.text:
Repository of transport-related discrete choice datasets designed to support reproducible research in discrete choice modelling, reinforcement learning, and machine learning workflows.

The repository provides standardized datasets, metadata, preprocessing pipelines, and reproducible formatting scripts for transport choice modelling research.

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text:
This repository aims to:

  1. Collect transport-related discrete choice datasets.
  2. Preserve links between datasets, publications, preprocessing decisions, and metadata.
  3. Provide reproducible Python pipelines for formatting raw datasets.
  4. Support discrete choice modelling workflows in:
  5. Apollo,
  6. Biogeme,
  7. and machine learning environments.
  8. Facilitate reproducible benchmarking and multi-task learning experiments.

docs.title: Dataset Inventory
docs.text: Dataset Folder Netherlands Mode Choice 1987_netherlands_modechoice UK Value of Time 1994_uk_vot Swissmetro 2001_swissmetro Norway VTT 2009_norway_vtt Arentze 2013_Arentze Spain Parking Choice 2014_spain_parkingchoice Netherlands Flight Choice 2016_netherlands_flightchoice Apollo Route Choice 2018_apollo_routechoice London Mode Choice 2018_london_modechoice OPTIMA Mode Choice 2018_optima_modechoice Taboo Dataset 2018_Taboo Apollo Mode Choice 2019_apollo_modechoice de Almeida Correia 2019_dealmeidacorreia Mouter Route Choice B 2019_mouter_routechoice_b Mouter Route Choice C 2019_mouter_routechoice_c Mouter Route Choice D 2019_mouter_routechoice_d van Cranenburgh VOT 2019_vancranenburgh_vot Death by Automation 2020_deathbyautomation Decisions Dataset 2020_decisions Street-Level Conditions 2026_cv_dcm

docs.title: Repository Structure
docs.text:
Each dataset follows a standardized directory structure.

datasets/
└── <dataset_slug>/
    ├── README.md
    ├── metadata.yaml
    │
    ├── raw/
    │   ├── dataset.yaml
    │   └── <dataset_slug>_original.<ext>
    │
    └── processed/
        ├── <dataset_slug>_formatted.csv
        ├── metadata.yaml
        └── <dataset_slug>_dictionary.csv

docs.title: Raw Data
docs.text:
The raw/ directory contains:

  • original dataset files,
  • source-specific formatting,
  • and the main dataset.yaml configuration file describing how the dataset should be processed.

docs.title: Processed Data
docs.text:
The processed/ directory contains:

  • formatted datasets,
  • metadata summaries,
  • and automatically generated variable dictionaries.

docs.title: Metadata
docs.text:
Each dataset includes metadata describing:

  • source publications,
  • authors,
  • choice structures,
  • alternatives,
  • attributes,
  • covariates,
  • and preprocessing operations.

docs.title: Processing Pipeline
docs.text:
The repository includes reproducible Python pipelines for:

  • loading raw data,
  • formatting datasets,
  • applying transformations,
  • generating metadata,
  • validating schemas,
  • and exporting standardized outputs.

docs.title: Process a Dataset
docs.text:
python scripts/build_dataset.py datasets/2001_swissmetro/raw/dataset.yaml

The pipeline:

  1. Loads raw data files.
  2. Applies preprocessing operations.
  3. Normalizes column names.
  4. Generates metadata summaries.
  5. Exports formatted datasets and dictionaries.

docs.title: Dataset YAML Configuration
docs.text:
Each dataset contains a dataset.yaml configuration file describing:

  • dataset metadata,
  • source information,
  • parsing rules,
  • data structure,
  • alternatives,
  • attributes,
  • covariates,
  • and preprocessing transformations.

The configuration system enables reproducible and transparent dataset formatting.

docs.title: Supported Transformations
docs.text:
The preprocessing pipeline supports:

  • row filtering,
  • column scaling,
  • recoding,
  • derived variables,
  • attribute aggregation,
  • binning,
  • quantile segmentation,
  • one-hot encoding conversion,
  • missing-value filtering,
  • and custom preprocessing operations.

docs.title: Example Workflow
docs.text:
A complete end-to-end example is available using the Swissmetro dataset.

examples/swissmetro_example.ipynb

The notebook demonstrates:

  • dataset loading,
  • preprocessing,
  • metadata generation,
  • and formatted dataset creation.

docs.title: Delphos Core
docs.text:
Welcome to the delphos-core repository, the heart of Multitask Delphos framework. This repository contains the complete reinforcement learning infrastructure required to:

  • formulate discrete choice model specification as a Markov Decision Process (MDP),
  • train reinforcement learning agents,
  • evaluate modelling strategies,
  • benchmark transferability across datasets,
  • and automate utility specification workflows.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text: The framework is organized into modular components.

docs.title: configs/
docs.text:
It defines the set of tasks to be used in the training process. Each discrete choice dataset can be defined as a task by creating a dataset.yaml file in dataset/ and registering it using task_registry.py.

Example:

from mdp.task import Task

TASKS: list[Task] = [
    Task.from_yaml(0, PROJECT_ROOT.parent / "dataset/dataset_1/dataset.yaml"),
    Task.from_yaml(1, PROJECT_ROOT.parent / "dataset/dataset_2/dataset.yaml"),
    Task.from_yaml(2, PROJECT_ROOT.parent / "dataset/dataset_3/dataset.yaml"),
    #...
    Task.from_yaml(N, PROJECT_ROOT.parent / "dataset/dataset_N/dataset.yaml"),
]

INFERENCE_TASKS: list[Task] = [
    Task.from_yaml(0, PROJECT_ROOT.parent / "dataset/dataset_N+1/dataset.yaml")
]

docs.title: mdp/
docs.text:
It defines defines all the core Markov Decision Process (MDP) components of Delphos. The specification process is formulated as a sequential decision-making problem in which:

  • states represent utility specifications,
  • actions represent modelling decisions,
  • and rewards evaluate the resulting estimated models.

docs.title: task.py
docs.text:
Defines the Task object, which acts as the central representation of a dataset and modelling environment. Responsible for:

  • parsing dataset.yaml files,
  • constructing attribute and covariate spaces,
  • generating structural tensors and masks,
  • and exposing environment configurations to the RL agent.

docs.title: state_manager.py
docs.text:
Defines the state space of all feasible model specification space given the attributes and covariates in the task object. It is responsible for:

  • encoding utility terms into symbolic states,
  • decoding specifications from symbolic states to utility structures,
  • generating fixed-length state representations.

docs.title: action_manager.py
docs.text:
Defines the modelling action grammar and valid actions available to the agent. It is responsible for:

  • adding utility terms,
  • modifying transformations,
  • changing taste structures,
  • adding covariate interactions,
  • and terminating specifications.

docs.title: task_runtime.py
docs.text: Encapsulates task instances, state managers, and action managers into a single environment instance.

docs.title: reward.py
docs.text:
Defines the reward functions used during training. Reward signals may incorporate:

  • AIC,
  • BIC,
  • Log-Likelihood,
  • adjusted rho-square,
  • behavioural plausibility,
  • convergence quality,
  • etc.

docs.title: env/
docs.text:
It connects Delphos with Apollo estimation software and is responsible for:

  • executing model estimations,
  • retrieving metrics,
  • caching results,
  • and coordinating communication between Python and the estimation backend.

docs.title: environment.py
docs.text:
Coordinates:

  • estimation requests,
  • reward retrieval,
  • caching,
  • and interaction between Delphos and the estimation environment.

docs.title: apollo_runner.py
docs.text:
Generates and executes Apollo estimation scripts dynamically. Responsible for:

  • Generating Apollo syntax to be ran using R,
  • launching estimation routines,
  • extracting estimation results,
  • and returning model metrics to the reinforcement learning environment.

docs.title: result_cache_sqlite.py
docs.text: Implements SQLite-based caching to avoid redundant model estimations.

docs.title: agent/
docs.text: It contains the reinforcement learning architectures and neural networks used by Delphos.

docs.title: delphos_agent.py
docs.text:
Includes:

  • policy network (a DQN),
  • modelling term embedding (a DeepSet encorder),
  • action masking,
  • exploration strategies,
  • multitask learning integration,
  • and state encoding pipelines.

docs.title: dqn.py
docs.text: Implements the Double Deep Q-Network (Double DQN) architecture used for training.

docs.title: z_state_encoder.py
docs.text: Contains permutation-invariant state encoders used to create a fixed-length state of model specifications.

docs.title: training/
docs.text:
It contains the training algorithm, where trainer.py:

Coordinates:

  • multitask training,
  • exploration,
  • replay buffer updates,
  • target network synchronization,
  • checkpointing,
  • and logging.

docs.title: transfer/
docs.text:
It contains benchmarking and evaluation workflows for unseen datasets. It supports:

  • zero-shot transfer,
  • few-shot adaptation.

docs.title: scripts/
docs.text:
Contains executable entry points for:

  • training (e.g., run_small_full_pipeline.py),
  • evaluation (e.g., run_evaluation.py),
  • benchmarking (e.g., run_transfer_benchmark.py).

Example:

python scripts/run_small_full_pipeline.py

docs.title: Training Delphos
docs.text: Training Delphos on a new discrete choice dataset involves three main steps.

docs.title: Step 1 — Define the dataset
docs.text:
Each dataset is configured through a dataset.yaml file.

The YAML configuration defines:

  • alternatives,
  • attributes,
  • covariates,
  • transformations,
  • baseline metrics,
  • and dataset paths.

Example:

alternatives:
  alt1:
    id: 1
    avail: "avail_1"

  alt2:
    id: 2
    avail: "avail_2"

attributes:
  time:
    id: 2
    mapping:
      alt1: "time_1"
      alt2: "time_2"

covariates:
  income:
    id: 1
    levels: [1, 2, 3]

ll_null: -1000.0
ll_linear: -800.0
n_obs: 2500

path_choice_dataset: "my_data_formatted.csv"

docs.title: Step 2 — Register the dataset as a Task object
docs.text:
Datasets are registered through the task registry.

Example:

from mdp.task import Task
from configs.task_configuration import PROJECT_ROOT

TASKS_YAML: list[Task] = [
    Task.from_yaml(
        0,
        PROJECT_ROOT.parent / "dataset/my_custom_dataset/dataset.yaml"
    ),
]

The framework automatically:

  • parses the YAML configuration,
  • generates masks,
  • builds state spaces,
  • and exposes the task to the RL environment.

docs.title: Step 3 — Run the Pipeline
docs.text:
Launch training through one of the scripts in scripts/.

Example:

python scripts/run_small_full_pipeline.py

docs.title: Training Workflow
docs.text: The training process follows three main stages.

docs.title: 1. Infrastructure Validation
docs.text:
Delphos validates:

  • dataset configurations,
  • state spaces,
  • action spaces,
  • and estimation environments.

docs.title: 2. Rollout Initialization
docs.text:
Random rollouts are executed to:

  • initialize caches,
  • validate Apollo integration,
  • and populate replay buffers.

docs.title: 3. Reinforcement Learning
docs.text: The agent progressively explores the specification space while receiving rewards from the estimation environment. Model estimations are executed dynamically using Apollo.
